title: Fix GoReleaser Build Version Injection
description: Released CLI binaries report version as dev instead of actual release version due to ldflags variable name mismatch and rootCmd.Version initialization timing issue.

title: US1: Released Binary Reports Correct Version
description: WHY: Released binaries always show dev instead of the actual tagged version, preventing users from verifying their installed version.
design: Two fixes required: (1) Update .goreleaser.yaml ldflags to reference main.buildVersion, main.buildCommit, main.buildDate instead of main.version, main.commit, main.date. (2) Move rootCmd.Version assignment into init() in cmd/sl/main.go after version package variables are set.
acceptance_criteria: Binary built via GoReleaser shows tagged release version, commit hash, and build date when running sl --version.

title: Fix GoReleaser ldflags variable names
description: WHY: GoReleaser ldflags reference main.version, main.commit, main.date but Go source declares buildVersion, buildCommit, buildDate. The linker silently ignores non-matching flags.
design: File: .goreleaser.yaml line 47. Change ldflags from: -X main.version={{.Version}} -X main.commit={{.Commit}} -X main.date={{.Date}} to: -X main.buildVersion={{.Version}} -X main.buildCommit={{.Commit}} -X main.buildDate={{.Date}}. Keep main.buildType=release as-is (already matches).
acceptance_criteria: GoReleaser ldflags reference main.buildVersion, main.buildCommit, main.buildDate matching the Go variable declarations in cmd/sl/main.go.

title: Fix rootCmd.Version initialization timing
description: WHY: rootCmd.Version is set in a package-level struct literal which evaluates before init() copies ldflags values to the version package. Result: rootCmd.Version is always dev even with correct ldflags.

items.item: Version field removed from rootCmd struct literal
rootCmd.Version set in init() after version.Version population
sl --version shows correct version in released binary

design: File: cmd/sl/main.go. Remove Version: version.GetVersion() from the rootCmd var declaration (around line 48). Add rootCmd.Version = version.GetVersion() inside the existing init() function after the version package variables are set (after line 29).
acceptance_criteria: rootCmd.Version is set inside init() after version package variables are populated from build-time ldflags.

title: US2: Update Checker Detects Correct Installed Version
description: WHY: The update checker always thinks an update is available because the binary reports dev, causing false update prompts on every sl doctor run.
design: No code changes needed for this story - it is automatically resolved by fixing the version injection in US1. The existing version comparison logic in pkg/version/checker.go correctly handles semantic version comparison; it just needs real version strings instead of dev.
acceptance_criteria: sl doctor correctly reports up-to-date when on latest release. sl doctor correctly identifies newer versions when outdated. dev builds are handled gracefully.

title: Verify GoReleaser snapshot build produces correct version
description: WHY: Need to validate that the ldflags fix and rootCmd.Version timing fix produce correct version output in the built binary.
design: Run goreleaser build --snapshot --clean to produce a test binary. Execute the built binary with --version flag. Verify output contains snapshot version string (not dev), commit hash, and build date.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot binary sl --version shows version other than dev. Commit hash and build date are populated. sl doctor version check works correctly with the new binary.
